title: FrameWalk Mobile
description: a modpack that makes your old android phone actually play minecraft java edition, made by ScrewedSause on yt and modrinth
body:
# **FrameWalk Mobile 🚀📱**
### Minecraft Java on Android, actually playable.
### Let's be real—trying to run Java Edition on a phone usually feels like watching a slideshow. FrameWalk Mobile is a specialized Fabric 1.21.11 modpack built to change that. Whether you’re on PojavLauncher or MojoLauncher, this pack is gutted and rebuilt to squeeze every possible frame out of budget ARM chips like the Helio P35 (Samsung A03s) in which if you try to open the normal 1.21.11 version on it... itll just give you a black screen and crash.


### Review from a friend with a budget phone

## _Anonymous~_     _"It was actually surprising how much this improved my performance, at First it wasn't that much of an improvement but when i adjusted settings i suddenly got over 300fps and an average of about 100fps which is incredible for my device which is an oppo a98 which has a 120hz display so it was stable to an extent, i used it using mojolauncher and also adjusted some settings inside mojolauncher itself (to disable the V-Sync thats forced by the launcher). At last i really enjoyed this Modpack and i recommend it for every mobile-java player."_
## 
# With V-Sync on Oppo a98
![With V-sync enabled on oppo a98](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/44605159414d5bcb0a11b03a03d479e043c714ed.jpeg)

# Without V-Sync on Oppo a98
![Without V-sync enabled on oppo a98](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/94f262b9e1358c26299d79112bf02ad47c2ac3a2.jpeg)

### 
# **⚠️ START HERE: Installation Guide (PojavLauncher Users)**
### If you are using MojoLauncher, you can just download/update directly in the app. If you're on Pojav, follow these steps to avoid a crash-loop:
### 
### Clean Slate: Open Pojav, run 1.21.11 vanilla once, and quit at the main menu.
### 
### Fabric Time: Run the 1.21.11 Fabric Installer .jar using the "Install a .jar" button in Pojav.
### 
### The Mod Dump: Use a file manager (turn on "Show Hidden Files") and move the mods from this pack into:
### Android > data > net.kdt.pojavlaunch > files > .minecraft > mods.
### 
# The Setup:

### Renderer: Set to Zink (use HolyGL if Zink acts weird).

### Resolution Scaler: Start at 50-60%. (If you want to go to 25% for max speed, go for it—I'm not gonna judge you).

### Memory: Set this to 1200MB - 1500MB. DO NOT give it all your RAM or Android will kill the app and you'll crash.

# 💻 The "Zero Stutter" JVM Arguments
### Standard Java isn't built for phones with 3GB of RAM. To stop those annoying "hiccups" when you break blocks or load chunks, go to Settings > Java Tweaks > JVM Arguments, clear the box, and paste this:


 -XX:+UseG1GC -XX:+ParallelRefProcEnabled -XX:MaxGCPauseMillis=50 -XX:+UnlockExperimentalVMOptions -XX:+DisableExplicitGC -XX:+AlwaysPreTouch -XX:G1NewSizePercent=20 -XX:G1MaxNewSizePercent=30 -XX:G1HeapRegionSize=8M -XX:G1ReservePercent=15 -XX:G1HeapWastePercent=5 -XX:G1MixedGCCountTarget=4 -XX:InitiatingHeapOccupancyPercent=15 -XX:G1MixedGCLiveThresholdPercent=90 -XX:G1RSetUpdatingPauseTimePercent=5 -XX:SurvivorRatio=32 -XX:+PerfDisableSharedMem -XX:MaxTenuringThreshold=1


That makes perfect sense! If you only want to replace that bottom half so you don't mess up the formatting of your installation guide and reviews at the top, you can just highlight the old changelog and paste this right over it.

Here is just that specific section, formatted exactly the way you had it:

# FrameWalk Mobile — The "Global Potato" Update (26.1.x)
### We survived the jump to the new Minecraft engine! This update is a "scorched earth" approach to optimization (you are going to have to get used to my english level of typing). We stress-tested this on actual Android hardware locked at 1.1GB of RAM, and it bolted firmly to 60 FPS.

## ➕ The New Gear
**Crash Assistant: Budget phones crash. Now, instead of throwing you to the home screen, the game will actually tell you exactly why it crashed so you can adjust your RAM limits.**

**FPS Reducer: Battery and thermal control. Your phone's CPU now gets micro-breaks when you are in menus to cool down, stopping thermal throttling.**

**Packet Fixer & Fast IP Ping: Brings server-grade network stability to mobile. If you are playing on spotty mobile data, this stops you from rubber-banding and timing out.**

**Better Block Entities: Swapped in to keep chests and complex blocks optimized and low-poly on the new engine.**

**C2ME & NoisiumForked: Rebuilt the multi-threaded terrain generation stack. Sprinting into new chunks will not melt your CPU anymore.**

**Particle Visor: A highly aggressive new particle culling replacement to stop the game from rendering hidden potion and torch effects.**

# ➖ The "Fat" we cut
**Removed: Methane, More Culling, ThreadTweak, Exordium, MemoryLeakFix, FastAnim, and Auto Render Distance. (These haven't caught up to 26.1 or aren't needed with the new Vulkan rendering APIs).**

**Ksyxis: Permanently removed. With the new 26.x rendering pipelines, it is officially obsolete.**

**Iris: Removed. Shaders are cool, but not on a budget phone trying to hit a stable 60 FPS.**

# 🎮 In-Game Settings for the Win
### Note: The pack now ships with a pre-configured options.txt file so you don't crash on your first boot!

Balanced (For 6GB+ RAM):

1. Render Distance: 6 - 8 Chunks

2. Simulation Distance: 5 - 8 Chunks

3. Graphics: Fast

4. Clouds: OFF

Extreme / Potato Mode (For 3GB-4GB RAM limits):

1. Render Distance: 2 - 4 Chunks

2. Smooth Lighting: OFF

3. Particles: Minimal

4. Mipmap Levels: 0

5. Biome Blend: 0

# 💬 Support
### Stuck? Crashing? Need a hug because your phone is on fire?
### Discord: screwedsause
### Feel free to hit me up if you need help getting FrameWalk Mobile running!
